tasks.description: Implement auto-decomposition after 5 rejections in orchestrator
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/orchestrator.ts: Add rejection counter to task state. After 5 consecutive quality gate rejections, automatically break task into 2-3 smaller subtasks with reduced scope. Preserve original task ID as parent reference. Reset counter on acceptance.

review.summary: Dual-rejected: Claude (25/100) + Codex (25/100). File is incomplete and truncated mid-implementation. Missing critical functionality including task breakdown logic, error handling completion, and proper Redis key formatting. | The implementation is incomplete and does not fulfill the task spec. It introduces a markdown code fence (invalid syntax), uses the wrong threshold, lacks any logic to break tasks into subtasks, and does not reset rejection counters on success.

issues.description: File is truncated and incomplete - cuts off mid-line in Redis key template literal
Missing the core task breakdown functionality - no implementation of breaking tasks into 2-3 subtasks after 5 rejections
Incomplete handleTaskRejection method - missing the actual task breakdown logic when TASK_REJECTION_THRESHOLD (5) is reached
Missing handleTaskSuccess method implementation - referenced but not defined
No test file provided - violates requirement for Jest unit tests
Subtask interface defined but never used in the incomplete implementation
Source file begins with a markdown code fence. Code fences are invalid syntax in TypeScript source files.
REJECTION_THRESHOLD is set to 2 instead of the specified 5 consecutive quality gate rejections.
No logic implemented to automatically break a task into 2–3 smaller subtasks when the rejection counter reaches the threshold. Task splitting requirements are entirely missing.
There is no reset of the taskState.rejectionCount on a 'completed' status result, as required by the spec.
The handleTaskRejection method is truncated and incomplete—missing the rest of the Redis key and any subtask creation logic.
No unit tests were provided or updated to cover the new rejection and subtask behavior.

tasks.description: Add cascade-blocking detection to quality gate
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/quality-gate-config.ts: Add check for rejection reason patterns indicating cascade blocks (e.g., 'depends on', 'waiting for', 'blocked by'). Flag these for auto-decomposition trigger instead of standard retry. Return specific error code for orchestrator to handle.

tasks.description: Wire cascade detection to auto-decomposition flow
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/orchestrator.ts: Connect quality gate cascade-block error codes to decomposition trigger. When detected, bypass normal retry logic and immediately decompose task. Log cascade detection events with parent task ID for monitoring.

tasks.description: Add task complexity validation bypass for decomposed subtasks
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/task-complexity-validator.ts: Add flag to skip complexity validation for tasks marked as auto-decomposed subtasks. These are by definition smaller than parent and should not trigger additional decomposition. Preserve validation for manually created tasks.

tasks.description: Add orchestrator metrics for rejection tracking
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/orchestrator.ts: Add counters for total rejections, cascade blocks detected, auto-decompositions triggered. Expose via existing logger at INFO level. Include task ID and rejection reason in each log entry for post-mortem analysis.
